summary:
Deep-learning models are widely applied in pharmacological research to predict molecular properties including stability and binding affinities; thus, predicting protein solubility through similar approaches is both feasible and consistent with established trends in AI-based drug development. The absence of an explicit reference does not negate this plausibility due to strong indirect support from related applications in medicinal chemistry.  

The summary should only contain two sentences focused solely on clinically significant facts or implications:

There exists a reasonable basis for believing that deep-learning algorithms can predict protein solubility based on their successful application in modeling other critical molecular traits during drug development. Protein solubility—a vital determinant of therapeutic efficacy—is routinely assessed computationally within modern pharmaceutical pipelines, supporting its inclusion as a target for predictive AI systems.
